preferences: Route by tier, not habit: Claude Opus is the implementation workhorse; Codex GPT (workhorse tier) is the default contrarian reviewer and design challenger.
Escalate to claude/claude-fable-5 ONLY when a problem already resisted a first attempt or is clearly gnarly architecture. Never for routine work — it is the most expensive tier.
Escalate to codex/gpt-5.5 (thinking xhigh) for hard contrarian analysis, high-stakes reviews, or when a review is repeatedly inconclusive. Ordinary reviews stay on gpt-5.4.
Cross-family pairing: work authored by a Claude agent gets reviewed by a Codex agent, and vice versa. Frontier-vs-frontier (Fable vs gpt-5.5) only to settle hard disagreements.
Downshift to claude/claude-haiku-4-5 or codex/gpt-5.4-mini for trivial mechanical work: test scaffolding, renames, format-only changes, log spelunking.
Always pass an explicit provider to create_agent. Never let an agent inherit or default its model.
Before spawning, state the chosen tier and a one-line reason (task difficulty class + role).
